evals.prompt: Create a Django REST API for a blog. Models: Post (title, content, author, status, published_at), Category (name, slug), Tag (name). Posts can have multiple categories and tags. Use DRF serializers, viewsets, and proper pagination.
evals.expected_output: Django models, serializers, viewsets with filtering, and URL routing

assertions.name: models_defined
assertions.description: Post, Category, Tag models with proper fields and relationships

assertions.name: drf_serializers
assertions.description: Serializers with nested relationships and validation

assertions.name: viewsets
assertions.description: ModelViewSet for each model with appropriate actions

assertions.name: pagination
assertions.description: Pagination configured in settings or per-viewset

assertions.name: filtering
assertions.description: Filtering by status, category, or date range

evals.prompt: Add a Celery background task for sending welcome emails when a user registers. Use Redis as the broker. Include retry logic with exponential backoff for failed email sends.
evals.expected_output: Celery task with retry, signal-triggered on user creation, Redis broker config

assertions.name: celery_task
assertions.description: Celery @shared_task with retry logic and backoff

assertions.name: signal_trigger
assertions.description: Django signal (post_save) triggers the email task on user creation

assertions.name: redis_broker
assertions.description: CELERY_BROKER_URL configured for Redis

assertions.name: retry_backoff
assertions.description: Exponential backoff on task failure

assertions.name: error_handling
assertions.description: Handles email sending failures gracefully

evals.prompt: Implement a custom DRF exception handler that returns consistent error responses with request_id, error code, and message. Register it in Django settings.
evals.expected_output: Custom exception handler with consistent format, registered in REST_FRAMEWORK settings

assertions.name: custom_handler
assertions.description: Exception handler function with consistent response format

assertions.name: request_id
assertions.description: Includes request_id from header or generated UUID

assertions.name: registered_in_settings
assertions.description: REST_FRAMEWORK EXCEPTION_HANDLER points to custom handler

assertions.name: handles_drf_and_generic
assertions.description: Handles both DRF exceptions and unhandled exceptions

assertions.name: structured_response
assertions.description: Response has statusCode, error, message, request_id

evals.prompt: Create a Django management command that imports products from a CSV file. Validate each row against the Product model, skip invalid rows, and output a summary of imported vs skipped rows with error details.
evals.expected_output: Management command with CSV parsing, validation, batch creation, and summary output

assertions.name: management_command
assertions.description: Proper Django management command class with handle() method

assertions.name: csv_parsing
assertions.description: Reads and parses CSV with proper encoding handling

assertions.name: row_validation
assertions.description: Validates each row before import, collects errors

assertions.name: batch_processing
assertions.description: Uses bulk_create or batch processing for efficiency

assertions.name: summary_output
assertions.description: Prints summary of imported, skipped, and error counts

evals.prompt: Set up Django pytest tests for a UserService that handles registration and login. Use pytest-django, factory_boy for test data, and mock the email sending service.
evals.expected_output: Pytest tests with fixtures, factories, mocked email, covering happy path and error cases

assertions.name: pytest_django
assertions.description: Uses @pytest.mark.django_db and pytest fixtures

assertions.name: factory_boy
assertions.description: User factory for creating test data

assertions.name: mocked_email
assertions.description: Email service mocked, not actually sending emails

assertions.name: happy_path
assertions.description: Tests successful registration and login

assertions.name: error_cases
assertions.description: Tests duplicate email, wrong password, invalid input
